subtitle: Between Acts
quote: One foot off the cliff, the other still wiping the counter.
format_note: BBS-aesthetic chrome — full diner scene with card preview, classified-ad layout, console hints. The most overtly meta of the cards so far.

ciphers.text: WELCOME TO RUST_CODE.BBS / GRAUSTARK_FRONTIER / ACTIVE NODES: 64
ciphers.encoding_hint: RUST_CODE.BBS is the in-fiction shared system the cards are nodes of. 64 active nodes = the chapter count when all volumes finish. Direct link to the Priestess card's NARRATIVE STRUCTURE COMPASS.

FRANK + LY = John Frank; FOOL + ISH = the arcana. The graffiti spells the chapter's title backwards through the character's name — diegetic-meta. Both painted in the same hand suggests author signature.

label II — but the FOOL is 0; this label points to the High Priestess (II) as the next door. The PRECIPICE DOOR is the threshold between actor and observer, and Elicia is the observer. The Fool's first step off the cliff is INTO Elicia's archive.
label IV → Emperor connection. DEMOSCENE_VIBE.EXE is the aesthetic engine of the project itself (matches the tarot_synth + ASCII walls). Dante's empire = the synchronization layer.

ciphers.text: small classic-tarot-style Fool card pinned at the upper-left, complete with own border + title and 'THE FOOL' label
ciphers.encoding_hint: the card-within-the-card shows the TRADITIONAL Fool iconography (cliff edge, sun, dog) while the surrounding scene shows the MODERN version (diner counter, jaundiced light). The recursion is the chapter's argument: every modern person occupies an ancient archetype.

ciphers.text: white-and-grey dog beside the counter — traditional Fool companion
ciphers.encoding_hint: the dog is the only character besides John in the scene. May be a named entity in vol5_ch0_booth6 prose — TBD. If unnamed, this is its first canonical visual.

ciphers.text: knotted bindle on a stick over John's shoulder
ciphers.encoding_hint: traditional Fool's bundle — anachronistic for a 1990s diner worker. The bindle being there at all signals the scene is operating in mythic time as much as clock time.

text_lines: Establishing shot: D'Ambrosio's interior, dawn-not-quite-dawn lighting
Frasier's entrance triggers first 'choice' beat — illusory
Color palette: jaundiced fluorescent yellow, bleeding shadow
ciphers.encoding_hint: VN DESIGN NOTES rendered IN the card — fourth wall. 'Frasier's entrance triggers first "choice" beat — illusory' is a player-facing tell that the first choice in vol5_ch0 doesn't matter, the path is fixed.

text_lines: THE CARD: The Fool stands at the precipice, knapsack on a stick, dog yapping at his heels. A sun outside the awnings: it sleeps. He's journey hasn't started; it's always about to.
UPRIGHT: Beginnings, naivete, free spirit, innocence, leap of faith, infinite potential, the witness who hasn't yet committed.
REVERSED: Recklessness, foolishness, paralysis disguised as openness, the 24-hour diner of the soul where you never actually leave.
ciphers.encoding_hint: Full oracle text printed in the card chrome. The REVERSED meaning ('24-hour diner of the soul where you never actually leave') is the chapter's true register — John has been at this counter long before the scene starts.
in-card UI affordances suggesting this scene IS a RPG/VN screen. Notebook + inventory + restart hint at the gameplay loop. weapon/shield slots labeled but empty — Fool starts with nothing.

ciphers.text: a red steamboat, reflected in the diner window — the same boat Frasier is modeling in the warehouse across town
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've found the steamboat on the Magician card. Frasier's model predicts the empire; this echo proves the prediction has already landed in John's peripheral vision. Two cards, one boat, one town.

ciphers.text: the wall clock above the door reads 3:47 AM
ciphers.encoding_hint: The clock will not move while the player is on this card. Only the painted shadow under it shifts, very slowly. The chapter is a held breath; the Fool's leap is canonically a pose, not an event. Examine it once and the held-breath read becomes visible the next time you enter the diner.

ciphers.text: the small bell on a coil above the door
ciphers.encoding_hint: Tapping the bell does nothing on first visit — the diner is too dead. After the Priestess's tapes are examined the bell answers with two distinct tones (open / close) that aren't the same chord. Elicia heard him leave; she has not yet heard him arrive anywhere. The asymmetry is the chapter.

ciphers.text: the dawn-jaundiced fluorescent strip along the ceiling tile seam
ciphers.encoding_hint: An ambient tick gates onto the soundtrack once noticed. The fluorescents are John's metronome — every time you return to the Fool card after noticing them, the tick is there under whatever else is playing.

ciphers.text: a cassette spine labeled 'JOHN_47AM_3' tucked behind the BBS console
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've examined Anya's tapes on the Priestess. Elicia has been recording the Fool too, has been for a while — and she shelves him next to Anya. '_3' means there are at least three tapes; the player only ever sees one. The Priestess archive is older than the chapter — the Fool was filed before he leapt.

ciphers.text: a second tiny card tucked behind the recursive Fool card on the diner wall — face-down, ornate pattern
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've attempted to flip the Lovers card on VI. The card-within-the-card on Fool now has its OWN refused pair tucked behind it — the recursion goes both ways. Every modern person occupies an ancient archetype AND every ancient archetype hides a refused union behind it.

ciphers.text: a small tag on the bindle reading AVDI ET TACE in the same dog-eared hand as the Hierophant's banner
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've read the Hierophant's motto. The seeker also obeys the rite. The Fool may be 0 but he wears the institution's instruction stitched into his bag — the deck's two ends meet on a luggage tag. AUDI ET TACE addresses Maya in the church; it addresses John in the diner; it addresses the player on whatever screen they're on. Three rooms, one command.

DORMANT until you've read the Chariot's sysop handle. The diner's BBS header has been the project's whole meta-system since the Fool's first frame. Now it carries an obituary. ACTIVE NODES dropped from 64 to 63 — the node count was a chapter count, the chapter count was a person count, the person count is now one short. The Fool wipes his counter inside a network that has been counting its dead all night.

ciphers.text: Faith's iris under the counter — same calm yellow ring as the lion's eye on Strength VIII
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until the player has recognized Faith's lineage on the Strength card. Three sizes, one creature, one eye: the dog at the Fool's feet, the sentinel at the Chariot wreck, the beast on the Strength riverbank. The whole arc is now legible in her sleeping face on this card — she's already the lion at 3:47 AM, just at the scale that fits the diner. The deck's longest-running animal is in the Fool's frame from the start.

hotspots.notes: with the soldering iron armed, clicking the IV - DEMOSCENE_VIBE.EXE label boots the engine — same render path Frasier built into the cathedral.
clicking the counter triggers a sound cue (rag on formica). Easter egg: 64 wipes = unlocks alt prologue (matches ACTIVE NODES: 64)

DORMANT: appears only after the player discovers the steamboat in Frasier's model city on the Magician card. A reflection of the same red boat is now visible in the diner window — the empire is already in John's peripheral vision.

the wall clock above the door reads 3:47 AM. No matter how long the player lingers it stays at 3:47 — only the painted shadow under the clock moves, very slowly. The Fool's pause is canonically the chapter's whole register.

the small bell on a coil above the door. Tapping it makes no sound on first visit — the diner is too dead. After the Priestess card's tapes are examined the bell answers with TWO distinct tones (one for opening, one for closing) that aren't the same chord — Elicia heard him leave, she has not yet heard him arrive anywhere.
the dawn-jaundiced fluorescent strip running along the ceiling tile seam. Notice it once and you'll start hearing its tick under the ambient track.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has examined the Anya tapes on the Priestess card. A small cassette spine labeled 'JOHN_47AM_3' is now visible tucked behind the BBS console on the counter — Elicia has been recording him too, has been for a while, and she shelves him next to Anya. The archive is older than the chapter.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has attempted to flip the Lovers card on VI. The recursive Fool card pinned on the diner wall now shows a SECOND tiny card tucked behind it — face-down, in the same ornate pattern as the Lovers card. The card-within-the-card now has its own refused pair.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read AVDI ET TACE on the Hierophant. A small tag on John's bindle now visibly reads the same two words in the same dog-eared hand — the seeker also obeys the rite. The Fool may be 0 but he still wears the institution's instruction stitched into his bag.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read the sysop handle on the Chariot card. The RUST_CODE.BBS / GRAUSTARK_FRONTIER header banner now scrolls a second line at boot: 'sysop ember.ash.rest.bbs: the_charioteer (D.O.A.) — ACTIVE NODES: 63.' The active node count drops by one. The diner's BBS already knows the Chariot crashed; John is wiping a counter inside a network that has been counting its dead all night.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has recognized Faith's lineage on the Strength card. The dog's iris under the counter is now drawn with the same calm yellow ring as the lion's. The Fool's companion is the Strength card's beast at the diner-table scale — three sizes, one creature, one eye. Faith's whole arc visible in her sleeping face.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has seen the dawn-lit diner corner on the World card. The 3:47 AM clock now visibly carries a faint smudge on its minute hand — the same smudge the World's dawn version carries on its 06:18. Same clock, same hand, eventually moved. 0 ↔ XXI reads both directions: World shows the clock at dawn, Fool shows the dawn already implicit at 3:47. The chapter that didn't move was the chapter waiting for the World to let it.

easter_eggs: title BBS 'RUST_CODE.BBS' is the in-fiction unifying system — every card is a node. The Priestess calls the same thing the NARRATIVE STRUCTURE COMPASS. Two names for the same meta-layer.
ACTIVE NODES: 64 — at full project completion the chapter count should hit 64
Graustark TX/LA' — slash suggests the town straddles state lines, or is fictional/uncertain. Possible boundary-narrative clue for vol6/7
3:47 AM = the chapter's actual time; the dawn-not-quite-dawn lighting in VN DESIGN NOTES is the lighting cue for vol5_ch0 scene art
VN DESIGN NOTES box explicitly tells the player that Frasier's entrance triggers 'illusory' choice — narrative determinism is in-text canon
graffiti hand: FRANKLY and FOOLISH share style — may appear elsewhere in vol5 graffiti scenes (warehouse, riverboat exterior)
